query:
Research the company "cokeflorida".

Research the company's recent financial performance and strategic initiatives: (1) Latest quarterly earnings results, any P&L misses or revenue shortfalls, and management commentary on performance gaps; (2) Recent CEO changes, new leadership announcements, or strategic reviews initiated; (3) Guidance revisions, pulled forecasts, or 2026 outlook changes; (4) Production/manufacturing capacity, platform launches (especially new or midsize platform activity), and any public statements about production growth rates; (5) Pricing changes, promotional activity, or commercial strategy shifts; (6) Distribution expansion or channel strategy updates; (7) Cost reduction or operational efficiency initiatives announced; (8) Organizational structure showing functional silos (separate media/marketing, production/operations, pricing/commercial, distribution teams); (9) Industry comparables around forecast accuracy, margin trends, and cross-functional optimization challenges. Focus on finding the specific P&L contradiction—where individual functions report success but total business results disappoint.

Focus on the last 6 months. Return a comprehensive summary with specific facts, numbers, and dates.
answer:
**Executive Summary**
Coca-Cola Beverages Florida (Coke Florida) is currently executing its "Next 10" growth strategy to become Florida's leading consumer packaged goods company, supported by significant infrastructure investments including a new $300 million Tampa facility. However, the company faces mounting financial pressures from a 40% tariff-driven increase in aluminum costs and leadership transitions, including the departure of its COO and a pending lawsuit against CEO Troy Taylor. While the franchisor, The Coca-Cola Company, recently reported a rare Q4 2025 revenue miss, specific internal P&L contradictions for Coke Florida were not present in the available data.

**Financial Performance & Market Pressures**
Coke Florida's financial footprint shows mixed indicators and significant external cost pressures:
*   **Revenue Projections:** The company projected $2.8 billion in revenue for 2025, with sparkling brands (e.g., Coca-Cola Classic, Sprite) expected to drive 65% of revenue and still beverages accounting for the remaining 35% [MatrixBCG](https://matrixbcg.com/blogs/how-it-works/cocacolaflorida). Another source reports the company's annual revenue at approximately $900.8 million with a workforce of 2,333 employees [Coca-Cola Beverages Florida: Revenue, Competitors, Alternatives](https://growjo.com/company/Coca-Cola_Beverages_Florida). 
*   **Cost Headwinds:** Coke Florida is currently navigating a 40% increase in aluminum costs due to tariffs imposed in 2025 [Jacksonville Coke bottler navigates tariff-driven cost increases while expanding Florida partnerships - Jacksonville Business Journal](https://www.bizjournals.com/jacksonville/news/2026/04/02/coke-florida-ceo-discusses-new-cans-tariffs-marr.html).
*   **Franchisor Performance:** The Coca-Cola Company (Coke Florida's franchisor) experienced its first quarterly revenue shortfall against Wall Street projections in five years during Q4 2025 [Coca-Cola is about to report earnings. Here's what to expect](https://www.cnbc.com/amp/2026/02/10/coca-cola-ko-q4-2025-earnings.html). Franchisor CEO James Quincey described the miss as a "weird anomaly" despite overall strong revenue growth [Coca-Cola (KO) Q4 2025 earnings](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/02/10/coca-cola-ko-q4-2025-earnings.html). Additionally, the franchisor's juice, value-added dairy, and plant-based beverage segment lagged in Q1 2026 with a 1% volume decline [Coca-Cola tops estimates, raises earnings outlook as global beverage demand rises](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/04/28/coca-cola-ko-q1-2026-earnings.html).
*   **Corporate Recognition:** Despite cost pressures, Coke Florida was named a 2026 US Best Managed Company for the fifth consecutive year, an award evaluating financial performance and strategic execution [Coke Florida Recognized as a US Best Managed Company](https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20260513455636/en/Coke-Florida-Recognized-as-a-US-Best-Managed-Company).

**Leadership Changes & Strategic Reviews**
The last six months have seen significant executive restructuring at both the bottler and franchisor levels:
*   **Coke Florida Executive Shifts:** Thomas Benford, President and COO, transitioned out of his role at the end of 2025 to become an advisor to CEO Troy Taylor [Coke Florida makes personnel changes | Vending Market Watch](https://www.vendingmarketwatch.com/beverage/news/55276577/coke-florida-makes-personnel-changes). On April 1, 2026, Reagan Calhoun was appointed Vice President and Chief of Staff to CEO Troy Taylor to provide financial leadership and operational insight [Coke Florida Promotes Calhoun VP, Chief Of Staff To CEO](https://theshelbyreport.com/2026/04/01/coke-florida-promotes-calhoun-vp-chief-of-staff-to-ceo/). Calhoun previously served as VP of Finance – Marketing for The Coca-Cola Company's North America Operating Unit [Reagan Calhoun Joins Coke Florida as Vice President and Chief of Staff - Coca-Cola Beverages Florida](https://cokeflorida.com/press-releases/reagan-calhoun-joins-coke-florida-as-vice-president-and-chief-of-staff/).
*   **Legal Challenges:** A lawsuit filed by a former Coke Florida executive against CEO Troy Taylor was cleared to move forward on March 24, 2026 [Jabil raises AI revenue target by $1B as data centers surge - Tampa Bay Business Journal](https://www.bizjournals.com/tampabay/news/2026/03/24/jabil-ai-data-centers-investments-us.html).
*   **Franchisor CEO Transition:** On March 31, 2026, Henrique Braun officially succeeded James Quincey as CEO of The Coca-Cola Company, with Quincey transitioning to Executive Chairman after nine years as CEO [Coca-Cola announces major leadership change as Henrique Braun is named next CEO](https://www.foxbusiness.com/markets/coca-cola-announces-major-leadership-change-henrique-braun-named-next-ceo). This transition is part of a broader strategy to accelerate digital transformation and technology adoption [The Coca-Cola Company Announces Changes in Operational Leadership and Creation of New Enterprise Role to Accelerate Digital Transformation](https://www.coca-colacompany.com/media-center/the-coca-cola-company-announces-changes-in-operational-leadership-and-creation-of-new-enterprise-role).

**Production Capacity & Strategic Initiatives**
Coke Florida has aggressively expanded its manufacturing and distribution footprint under its new strategic framework:
*   **"Next 10" Strategy:** Launched in September 2025, this initiative focuses on accelerated growth to make Coke Florida the #1 consumer packaged goods company in the state [PR Newswire](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/from-milestone-to-momentum-coke-florida-embarks-on-ambitious-next-10-strategy-for-growth-302549432.html). As part of this, the company appointed four new Region General Managers to lead newly established operating regions [ZoomInfo](https://www.zoominfo.com/c/coke-florida/369723772).
*   **Capacity Expansion:** In May 2025, the company opened a $300 million+, 156-acre facility in Tampa featuring an 800,000-square-foot fully automated warehouse and a 250,000-square-foot fleet maintenance facility [Business Wire](https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20250530238512/en/Coke-Florida-Hosts-Grand-Opening-for-New-Tampa-Distribution-Center). The Tampa manufacturing facility produces 21 million cases annually and operates seven days a week [Florida Trend](https://www.floridatrend.com/article/20367/coca-cola-beverages-florida-its-about-the-brand/). 
*   **Platform Launches:** The Jacksonville manufacturing facility was selected to produce limited-edition 'America250' commemorative Coca-Cola cans representing all 50 states [Business Wire](https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20250530238512/en/Coke-Florida-Hosts-Grand-Opening-for-New-Tampa-Distribution-Center).
*   **Operational Efficiency:** Between 2024 and 2025, Coke Florida implemented BIOIONIX technology to save up to 90% of bottle and can warmer process water for reuse [LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/company/cokeflorida). Currently, 75% of its manufacturing facilities are GreenCircle Certified, with the Broward location holding a Zero Waste to Landfill certificate [Business Wire](https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20220829005397/en/75-of-Coke-Floridas-Manufacturing-Facilities-Are-GreenCircle-Certified).

**Distribution & Channel Strategy**
As the sixth-largest independent Coca-Cola bottler in the U.S., Coke Florida serves an exclusive territory of 47 counties, reaching over 21 million consumers daily with more than 600 refreshment options [Forbes](https://www.forbes.com/companies/coca-cola-beverages-florida/) | [Coca-Cola Brands & Products - Coca-Cola Beverages Florida](https://cokeflorida.com/products/). 
*   **Logistics Footprint:** The company was ranked the 9th largest private beverage carrier in the U.S. for 2025 by Transport Topics [About Us & Our Story - Coca-Cola Beverages Florida](https://cokeflorida.com/about/). 
*   **Network Expansion:** Beyond its four primary manufacturing facilities (Tampa, Orlando, Jacksonville, Hollywood), Coke Florida has built a new facility in St. Petersburg and purchased or expanded facilities in Jacksonville, Orlando, Ocala, Sarasota, and Winter Haven [Florida Trend](https://www.floridatrend.com/article/19906/coca-cola-beverages-florida-to-acquire-four-production-facilities-from-the-coca-cola-company/) | [Business Wire](https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20250530439105/en/Coca-Cola-Beverages-Florida-Celebrates-10-Years-of-Growth-Innovation-and-Community-Impact).

**Gaps and Limitations**
The provided research findings do not contain information to address several specific elements of the query:
1.  **P&L Contradiction:** There is no data indicating a specific internal P&L contradiction where individual Coke Florida functions reported success while total business results disappointed.
2.  **Guidance Revisions:** No information was found regarding pulled forecasts, guidance revisions, or specific 2026 outlook changes for Coke Florida.
3.  **Pricing & Commercial Strategy:** Aside from the 40% aluminum cost increase, there are no details on specific pricing changes, promotional activities, or commercial strategy shifts.
4.  **Organizational Silos:** The findings do not detail the internal organizational structure regarding functional silos (separate media/marketing, production/operations, pricing/commercial, distribution teams).
5.  **Industry Comparables:** No data was provided regarding industry comparables for forecast accuracy, margin trends, or cross-functional optimization challenges.
